title: Kitchen Cabinet Pull Out Shelves
summary: Get your kitchen cabinets organized with these easy DIY kitchen cabinet pull out shelves made with plywood and pocket holes.
description: Get your kitchen cabinets organized with these easy DIY kitchen cabinet pull out shelves made with plywood and pocket holes.

materials.name: Pocket hole screws (1" zinc coarse) - see tools list
Drawer slides (18” full extension side mount, 50-lb capacity)
Heavy-duty drawer slides (100-lb capacity) for mixer shelf

steps.title: Cut wood
steps.body: Cut the plywood panel down to size according to the visual cut list in the woodworking plans. We used the Kreg Adapative Cutting System to cut everything down to size. You could also use a table saw or Rip-Cut for this.

steps.title: Drill pocket holes
steps.body: Drill pocket holes on the wood where shown in the woodworking plans. We used the Kreg Rebel.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body: Attach the bottoms to the side pieces for the drawers and bases using the pre-drilled pocket holes and 1” pocket hole screws. Since we’re only working with ½” plywood, it is important not to overtighten the pocket hole screws because you’ll go right through the soft plywood. Attach the front and back pieces on the drawers with wood glue and clamp until dry. Once dry, add pin nails to the joints on the front and back pieces. Attach the tower support piece with pin nails.

steps.title: Sand
steps.body: Sand everything smooth with 120-grit and 220-grit sandpaper and an orbital sander.

steps.title: Fill
steps.body: Fill the pin nail holes, joints, and imperfections with wood filler and let dry. Then sand smooth.

steps.title: Seal
steps.body: Spray with lacquer and let dry. If you wanted to stain or paint the pull out shelves, you could do that as well, but we opted to keep ours natural.

steps.title: Attach drawer slides
steps.body: Attach the drawer slides to the side pieces on each drawer & the bases according to the manufacturer's instructions. We used heavy-duty drawer slides on the mixer drawer that can withstand up to 100 pounds because the stand mixer is fairly heavy.

steps.title: Install in kitchen cabinets
steps.body: Install inside your kitchen cabinet using wood shims or blocks if necessary. Secure with 1 ½” trim screws on the base and one side of the tower.
